codeReview.summary: The fix does not satisfy the original blocking requirement. It removes the derive-emitted helper guard and adds a heuristic uppercase-name check, but the non-Store-child contract still fails through downstream runtime bounds and helper APIs instead of one stable derive-time diagnostic. That means the original failure remains, and the new heuristic also risks rejecting valid Store children whose type names are lowercase or type-aliased.

issues.description: The refreshed snapshot is still dominated by downstream `ResolveRecordId`, `Crud`, and `Repo::<PlainChild>::get_record` trait-bound errors rather than a single focused derive-time diagnostic about accepted `#[store(ref)]` shapes and Store-child requirements. This does not resolve the original scrutiny failure.

`looks_like_store_model_type` decides whether a nested-ref child ‘must derive Store’ purely by checking whether the last path segment starts with an uppercase ASCII letter. That is not a semantic Store check: valid Store models referenced through lowercase type aliases or modules can be rejected, while non-Store uppercase types still pass through to downstream bounds. The fix therefore introduces an unsound heuristic instead of the allowed canonical marker-trait seam.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill now says derive-time validation should keep compile-fail stderr focused on the derive contract, but this fix handoff still reported success after landing a snapshot that remains downstream-bound-heavy. The procedure should explicitly require reviewers/workers to inspect the final stderr text against the feature requirement, not just that trybuild passes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill: C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\skills\rust-library-worker\SKILL.md step 4; handoff C:\Users\admin\.factory\missions\802cdcc2-45d2-48bc-8766-b8d7e8118a35\handoffs\2026-03-21T07-12-24-129Z__nested-store-macro-contract-diagnostics-fix__d5830981-887c-48f5-9948-d197784e7a35.json marks success, but core/tests/ui/store/nested_ref_non_store_child.stderr still shows downstream `ResolveRecordId`, `Crud`, and `Repo` errors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission/library guidance already says a narrow exported or `#[doc(hidden)]` Store marker trait is allowed as the canonical diagnostic seam for nested refs, but the worker substituted a name-shape heuristic instead. That suggests the convention should stress that semantic marker seams are preferred over lexical heuristics whenever the requirement is to prove a derive contract.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\library\nested-store.md explicitly allows a marker-trait seam; macros/src/lib.rs:778-786 implements `looks_like_store_model_type` using uppercase-name detection instead.

summary: Reviewed the original failing feature commit `d2df10e` and the fix commit `cde03d7b43edc6b0de6fa5557664e63750fe27f3`, along with both fix handoffs, transcript skeleton, mission guidance, and current code. The fix does not adequately address the original failure: the compile-fail contract is still noisy and downstream-coupled, and the new uppercase-name heuristic is a new blocking correctness risk. Report remains fail.
